title: Living in Boulder vlog 11 -  8 -  2017: Finished Google Earthing Battles, now linking 1900s battles.

enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a late-night vlog update (1 AM on Nov 9, 2017) about his progress on a personal project linking historical battles to a timeline. He finished cataloging 1900s and 2000s battles using Google Earth, is now in the process of linking ~560 battles with photos, and looks ahead to finishing castles and dukes sections before returning to a Roman history project.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: satisfied with finishing the 1900s/2000s battles phase, optimistic about completing all battle links by Sunday
finds car review content (Doug DeMuro-style) entertaining as a casual watch
thinks comparing cars by strength-to-weight parity rather than raw speed would be interesting

transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder, Vlog. It's 2.51 a.m. on November 9th, so I guess this will be the one for the November 8th.
So I got, I was working really late last night, until like 6 in the morning today, or yesterday, and so when I got up, it wasn't until like noon or 1 or something, I got a full 8 hours of sleep.

1 or 2 or something, or more. Didn't get started until late, but yeah, I went, I can't remember when the last, the last time I did it was like 2 days ago, and I was at like, I can't remember where I was with battles, it was like somewhere in the 1800s or something.

Anyways, today I finished battles, with the 1900s and the 2000s, and then I finished making the links, so right now I'm linking the battles to my timeline, and then I got to 100, the first 100, and there's like 560 battles.
Hmm, we're hidden, there's lots of photos, so it's like a thousand, you know, pictures, because some have a bunch of photos, that I have to link.
Um, and then there's the battles for the 2000s, so yeah, but that's cool, I'm like ahead of schedule, so.
Um, yeah.
So yeah, maybe by Sunday I'll be done linking all of the battles, or maybe, yeah, earlier, and that'll make me feel like I should go ahead and just,
Do castles and cathedrals, um, before I have to do it all from the beginning, though, I have to do all the steps.
I already have the photos on Flickr, but, yeah, because if I do that, if I finish those, then I can leave my timeline alone for a while,
And it'll be a great place to start again.
Um, and then I'll go back to, um, uh, Google Earth in Rome.
I really want to see what Rome looks like, uh, back in the day.
Hmm, I'll say my videos will be super fun.
But yeah, I should probably, um, go to sleep soon.
Uh, yeah, I haven't really been able to listen, interested in listening to history lately.
I was trying to listen to some Libby today, you know, I want to focus on Libby and memorize all that, but to do that, I want to, uh,
Put the photos on the side to help me memorize it.
But, um, anyway, uh, so I was listening to car reviews.
Doug something, Doug DeMuro, he has like a million subscribers.
He reviews sports cars and talks about them.
And then I found some other channel where some dude was reviewing old cars.
Yeah, I want to, uh, for my, uh, bike racer level page, I want to, uh, have cars for different bike racer levels based on strength per weight ratio.
Not like ones that go the same speed, but as far as strength per weight makes them kind of a parody.
That would be interesting.
Anyways, I think I've talked long enough to, uh, so yeah, tomorrow, what do you think I'll do tomorrow?
Maybe by tomorrow I can finish linking all of them.
That'd be awesome if I could link all of the battles to my timeline tomorrow and get started on castles and cathedrals.
Well, let's see, let's see what you do.
Okay, peace out.
